summary: Re-converged ADR 0096 on monitoring-lv3 from the integrated 0.143.0 mainline, verified blackbox-backed Prometheus SLO rule loading and the LV3 SLO Grafana dashboard, and refreshed the public Grafana edge controls required by the monitoring verification gate.

verification.observed: `make live-apply-service service=grafana env=production` completed successfully on 2026-03-25 with the monitoring role, Alertmanager runtime, and health-probe verification all passing on `monitoring-lv3`.

`systemctl is-active lv3-prometheus grafana-server prometheus-blackbox-exporter prometheus-alertmanager` returned four `active` states on `monitoring-lv3`, and `promtool check rules` reported `SUCCESS: 90 rules found` for `/etc/prometheus/rules/slo-rules.yml` plus `SUCCESS: 30 rules found` for `/etc/prometheus/rules/slo-alerts.yml`.

`https://grafana.example.com/api/health` now returns `404`, while `https://grafana.example.com/d/lv3-slo-overview/lv3-slo-overview` returns `302` to `/login?redirectTo=...`, matching the repo-managed ADR 0140 verification contract used by the ADR 0096 monitoring apply.

notes: The live apply refreshed the controller-local ntfy prerequisite in the same turn with `make converge-ntfy` before the successful monitoring converge.

A full `public-edge` converge in this fresh worktree stalled during the static-site copy phase, so the Grafana edge fix was applied from repo state with a narrower rerender path starting at `Check whether the public edge certificate exists`; this still rendered the managed NGINX config, validated it, and reloaded `nginx-lv3` successfully.
